project.name: ipad-as-display
project.description: Extends a Windows desktop to an iPad over Wi-Fi or USB as a second monitor. Screen is captured, JPEG-encoded, and streamed to a React PWA running in iPad Safari. Touch events are forwarded back as mouse/keyboard input via WebSocket.

entry_points.server: server/main.py — `python server/main.py` starts uvicorn on HOST:PORT (default 0.0.0.0:8080). Spawns capture_loop in a daemon thread.
entry_points.client_dev: client/ — `npm run dev` for Vite dev server
entry_points.client_build: client/ — `npm run build` outputs to client/dist/, served by FastAPI StaticFiles at /
entry_points.setup: setup_and_run.bat — installs deps, builds React, starts server
entry_points.run: run.bat — starts server (assumes deps already installed)

responsibilities: Mounts client/dist as static files at /
Prints startup banner with IP, QR URL, PIN
Loads startup config: defaults → config.local.json → env vars
Holds latest JPEG frame (bytes) with frame_id and stats
Holds runtime_config with config_version for cache-busting
Thread-safe via threading.Lock for frame, config, streams, stats
state.py.note: No database. All state is in-process. Restarting the server resets all state except persisted config.

responsibilities: Generates SESSION_TOKEN (urlsafe, 24 bytes) and PAIRING_PIN (6-digit) at startup
require_token() raises HTTP 401 for invalid tokens
selected_monitor(): resolves monitor_index to mss monitor dict
monitor_list(): returns all monitors with dimensions and aspect ratio
resolution_advisory(): computes aspect mismatch % between monitor and iPad viewport, recommends resolutions
pairing_url(ip, port, pin) → URL with ?pin= param
pairing_page(pin, url) → full HTML page with QR and PIN display
capture_loop(): tries dxcam_capture_loop(), falls back to mss_capture_loop() on import error or exception
encode_frame(): resizes PIL image if scale<1, encodes to JPEG bytes, returns (bytes, encode_ms)
publish_frame(): calls encode_frame, calls state.set_latest_frame with stats
Uses dxcam for low-latency DirectX screen grab
Re-initializes camera on config version change (monitor switch, settings change)
Calls publish_frame per tick, sleeps to maintain target FPS
capture/dxcam_capture.py.note: Maps monitor_index to dxcam output_idx as (monitor_index - 1)

responsibilities: Same loop structure as dxcam but uses mss.grab()
No camera object to manage; lighter but higher latency than DXGI

endpoints: GET /pair-qr — HTML page with QR code and PIN
POST /pair {pin} → {token} — validates PIN, returns session token
GET /settings?token&viewport_width&viewport_height → full settings + monitors + advisory + stream_stats
GET /stats?token → fps, quality, scale, auto_quality, active_streams, stream_stats, delivery_stats
PATCH /settings?token body:{partial config} → updated settings response
GET /stream?token — MJPEG multipart/x-mixed-replace stream (HTTP, polling latest frame)
WS /frames?token — JPEG binary WebSocket stream (one frame per message, rate-limited to min(30, fps))
WS /ws?token — receives JSON input events from iPad
routes/websocket.py.note: Refreshes monitor dimensions on config version change without reconnect

responsibilities: ScreenVideoTrack: grabs frames via mss, scales with PIL, wraps as av VideoFrame
prefer_h264(): sets H.264 as preferred codec via RTCRtpSender capabilities
Cleans up peer connection on failed/closed/disconnected state
routes/webrtc.py.optional_deps: aiortc, av, numpy — server returns HTTP 501 if not installed

responsibilities: Routes between PairingScreen (no token) and main display (token present)
Passes props down to DisplaySurface, Hud, SettingsPanel, SidecarControls, ConnectionOverlay

stream_mode_cascade: 2. JPEG WebSocket (WS /frames) — fallback on WebRTC failure
3. MJPEG HTTP (GET /stream) — final fallback on WS failure
responsibilities: Blob URL management for JPEG frames (rAF-throttled)
Connects to WS /ws, auto-reconnects every 2s on close
Auto-quality down: encode_ms>28 OR size>900KB → reduce fps/quality/scale, 8s cooldown
Auto-quality up: encode_ms<14 AND size<350KB for 20 consecutive samples → increase fps/quality/scale, 12s cooldown
Acquires navigator.wakeLock.request('screen') when token present and stream live
Returns {w, h} from window.visualViewport or window.innerWidth/Height
Renders
Handles all touch events: tap→click, long-press→rightclick, drag→mousemove, two-finger-scroll, pinch, three-finger gestures
Maps touch coordinates to normalized [0,1] space relative to displayBox
Shows stream mode (WebRTC/JPEG-WS/MJPEG), WS status, latency, FPS, frame size
Settings button, Reconnect button, hide HUD button
Touch input, touch cursor, scroll speed, pinch zoom, three-finger gesture toggles

components/SidecarControls.jsx.role: On-screen sidecar sidebar and touch bar
responsibilities: Configurable position (left/right/off for sidebar, top/bottom/off for touchbar)
Does not block touch events when live
Shows pairing errors and busy state
Renders a dot at touch position when touch_cursor enabled
getDisplayBox(displayInfo, viewportSize, viewMode) → {x,y,w,h,scaleX,scaleY} for coordinate mapping

steps: 1. capture_loop (daemon thread): dxcam.grab() or mss.grab() → PIL Image
2. capture/base.publish_frame(): resize (if scale<1) + JPEG encode → bytes + stats
3. state.set_latest_frame(): stores bytes + frame_id + stats under frame_lock
4a. WebRTC path: ScreenVideoTrack.recv() pulls from mss directly (independent loop), wraps as av.VideoFrame → aiortc sends via RTP
4b. JPEG WS path: /frames WS sends bytes to connected client at target FPS
4c. MJPEG path: /stream generator yields multipart boundary + JPEG bytes
1. DisplaySurface.jsx: touch event → normalize to [0,1] relative to displayBox
2. useWebSocketInput.send(): serialize to JSON, send on WS /ws
3. server/routes/websocket.py: parse JSON, check config version, compute abs_x/abs_y
4. pyautogui: moveTo / mouseDown / mouseUp / click / rightClick / scroll / hotkey / press / write

settings_sync.description: Bidirectional settings between iPad and server
steps: 1. iPad opens app → useDisplaySettings.loadSettings() → GET /settings?viewport_width=&viewport_height=
2. Server: monitors.settings_response() assembles config + monitors + advisory + stream_stats
3. iPad user changes setting → applySettings(patch) → PATCH /settings body:{key:val}
4. Server: state.update_config() validates + updates runtime_config + increments config_version + saves config.local.json
5. Capture loop detects config version change on next tick → reinitializes capture parameters
1. Server prints PIN at startup (6-digit random)
2. User opens /pair-qr on PC → scans QR with iPad camera → opens app URL with ?pin= prefilled
3. iPad → POST /pair {pin} → server validates with secrets.compare_digest → returns {token}
4. iPad stores token in localStorage (TOKEN_KEY)
5. All subsequent requests include ?token= query param
1. useLiveStats polls GET /stats every 1s
2. If auto_quality enabled and overloaded (encode_ms>28 OR size>900KB): PATCH /settings {fps:≤30, quality:≤75, scale:≤0.75}, 8s cooldown
3. If 20 consecutive stable samples (encode_ms<14 AND size<350KB): PATCH /settings {fps+5, quality+5, scale+0.05}, 12s cooldown

api_endpoints.description: QR code page for iPad scanning
Full settings + monitors list + resolution advisory + frame stats
Update one or more config keys, persists to config.local.json
Live perf: fps, quality, scale, active_streams, frame encode stats, delivery stats
MJPEG multipart HTTP stream — fallback streaming mode
Binary JPEG WebSocket stream — secondary streaming mode
Input event receiver: mouse, keyboard, scroll, pinch, gestures
WebRTC SDP offer/answer — primary streaming mode (H.264)

client_to_server.description: dy is raw pixel delta; server converts to pyautogui scroll clicks
Array of key names for pyautogui.hotkey()

server_to_client.description: Response to ping, used for RTT measurement

architecture_decisions.decision: Stream mode cascade: WebRTC → JPEG WS → MJPEG
architecture_decisions.rationale: WebRTC gives H.264 hardware encoding and lowest latency. JPEG WS avoids HTTP connection overhead vs MJPEG. MJPEG is the universal fallback that works on any browser.
architecture_decisions.implication: Client must manage three distinct connection paths and their lifecycle

architecture_decisions.rationale: Avoids external dependencies (Redis, DB) for a single-user local tool. Simple threading.Lock is sufficient.
architecture_decisions.implication: Server restart resets all in-flight state. Not scalable beyond one session.

architecture_decisions.rationale: No point capturing frames when no client is watching. Saves CPU.
architecture_decisions.implication: First frame after reconnect may be slightly delayed while capture restarts

architecture_decisions.rationale: Capture loop and WebSocket input handler both need to react to settings changes (monitor switch, scale change). Version bump is detected on next loop tick without a separate event/queue.
architecture_decisions.implication: All hot-path loops must call get_config() on every tick

architecture_decisions.decision: Token passed as ?token= query param (not Authorization header)
architecture_decisions.rationale: WebSocket connections and MJPEG cannot set custom headers. Query param is the only portable option.
architecture_decisions.implication: Token visible in server logs and browser history. Acceptable for local LAN use.

architecture_decisions.rationale: Decouples iPad viewport size from Windows monitor resolution. Server recomputes absolute coords from monitor dimensions.
architecture_decisions.implication: WebSocket handler must know current monitor dimensions; refreshed on config_version change

architecture_decisions.rationale: dxcam uses DXGI (DirectX desktop duplication) — significantly lower latency and CPU than GDI-based mss, especially at 30-60 fps.
architecture_decisions.implication: dxcam maps monitor by output index (0-based) not mss index (1-based): output_idx = monitor_index - 1

architecture_decisions.decision: React PWA with no CSS framework
architecture_decisions.rationale: Keeps bundle minimal for faster load on iPad. All styles are inline.
architecture_decisions.implication: No design system; all component styles are co-located inline objects

architecture_decisions.decision: Auto-quality runs entirely on the client
architecture_decisions.rationale: Avoids server-side logic that would require knowing client conditions. Client observes encode_ms and frame size from /stats, decides when to throttle.
architecture_decisions.implication: Auto-quality has 1s polling lag. Two iPads connected could fight over quality settings.

constraints: Windows-only: dxcam and pyautogui rely on Win32 APIs. mss also supports macOS/Linux but capture and input injection would need replacement.
Single-session: SESSION_TOKEN is one token for all clients. Multiple iPads share the same token.
No HTTPS by default: Wake Lock and Clipboard API require secure context (HTTPS or localhost). On LAN via IP, HTTPS would need a self-signed cert.
iPad Safari touch events only: no mouse events on iPad; all input must go through the touch handler in DisplaySurface.jsx.
pyautogui.PAUSE=0 is set globally: eliminates the 0.1s default delay between actions but removes all safety pauses.
WebRTC ScreenVideoTrack uses its own mss instance, not the shared capture loop: runs at display framerate, not config fps.
aiortc is optional: server runs without it (HTTP 501 on /webrtc/offer), falling back to JPEG WS / MJPEG.
config.local.json is not multi-process safe: atomic tmp-rename write mitigates single-process races but would fail under multi-worker uvicorn.

open_todos.description: Copy/paste between iPad and Windows host. PC→iPad: read Windows clipboard (pyperclip/win32clipboard), send text over WebSocket to client, write to navigator.clipboard. iPad→PC: navigator.clipboard.readText() on user gesture (HTTPS required), send via WebSocket typewrite or new clipboard_push event.
blockers: navigator.clipboard.readText requires HTTPS on real LAN IPs
Clipboard write on iPad requires user gesture

open_todos.description: Guided wizard in SettingsPanel when resolution_advisory.status == 'warning'. Steps: 1) link to Virtual Display Driver install, 2) show recommended resolutions for this iPad viewport, 3) Windows Display Settings instructions. Backend already provides advisory.recommended_resolutions and advisory.mismatch_percent.
existing_hooks: monitors.resolution_advisory() already computes mismatch_percent and recommended_resolutions
SettingsPanel already renders the advisory block

file_map.server/main.py: App factory, startup banner, capture thread
file_map.server/state.py: In-memory frame buffer, config, stream/connection counters
file_map.server/auth.py: Session token, PIN generation, rate limiting
file_map.server/monitors.py: Monitor discovery, resolution advisory, settings payload assembly
file_map.server/qr.py: Dependency-free QR SVG + pairing page HTML
file_map.client/src/App.jsx: Root component, hook wiring, render tree
file_map.client/src/api.js: fetch/WebSocket URL builders, typed API calls
